deck: Simeon Yosefe, Malawi's Head of Digital Health, joins Jonathan Jackson to share hard-won guidance for technologists in global health: align to the government's vision, build for the long term, and replace siloed vertical systems with comprehensive, sustainable ones.
seo_description: Simeon Yosefe, Malawi's Head of Digital Health, on aligning partners to government strategy and replacing siloed systems with comprehensive digital health.
og_description: Malawi's Head of Digital Health, Simeon Yosefe, on building comprehensive, sustainable digital health systems.

pull_quote.text: For us as a country, we want to implement and deploy comprehensive systems. A system that actually encompasses everything. HIV may be a starting point, but we want these resources not only to be limited to HIV, but they should cover everything.
pull_quote.cite: Simeon Yosefe, on building comprehensive systems

bullets: From statistician to Head of Digital Health: Simeon Yosefe's path into transforming how Malawi manages health data
40 systems and counting: an assessment that surfaced duplicated, siloed, partner-named tools, many already abandoned
Harmonizing work plans: pooling partner priorities to see where resources cluster and where critical needs go neglected
Infrastructure over dongles: pushing partners away from short-term fixes toward long-term connectivity investments
Policy with teeth: drafting an enforcement framework so alignment survives when grant deliverables conflict
Beyond the vertical program: caring for the whole patient who may have HIV, TB, and a pregnancy at once
Shared value: how to reevaluate whether a multi-year digital investment is delivering the health outcome it promised

stats.label: different digital health systems found running across Malawi during the landscape assessment

guests.name: Simeon Yosefe
guests.role: Head of Digital Health, leading the effort to align partners to national strategy and build comprehensive, sustainable systems. A statistician by training.
